description: A write updates the source but not the cache, so reads serve stale data until the TTL eventually expires.
body: Failure mode: data is cached on read but writes to the source do not delete the cached key, so every read returns the old value for the full TTL window. Detection signals: updates not visible until the cache entry expires; no DEL on the write path; bug severity scales with TTL length. Do not lengthen TTL or rely on it to self-heal. Safer fallback: on every source write, delete the precise derived cache keys (delete rather than update to avoid races), and choose key names so writes can compute which keys to invalidate.
